# @zakkster/lite-headless/command-palette

Headless command palette: a registry of invocable commands with
fuzzy filtering, ARIA combobox keyboard nav, and a global Cmd+K /
Ctrl+K binding to toggle visibility. Designed to **compose with
a dialog** — the palette doesn't duplicate modal / focus-trap
work; the consumer wires the open/close events to their dialog
primitive.

## Quick example

```js
import { createCommandPalette } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/command-palette";
import { createDialog }         from "@zakkster/lite-headless/dialog";

const dialog  = createDialog({ modal: true });
const palette = createCommandPalette({
    triggerKey: { key: "k", meta: true },   // Cmd+K / Ctrl+K
    onOpen:     () => dialog.open(),
    onClose:    () => dialog.close(),
});

palette.attachInput(document.getElementById("cmd-input"));
palette.attachList (document.getElementById("cmd-list"));
palette.attachEmpty(document.getElementById("cmd-empty"));

palette.register([
    { id: "save",  label: "Save",  keywords: ["write"], onSelect: () => save() },
    { id: "open",  label: "Open",                       onSelect: () => openFile() },
    { id: "find",  label: "Find",  keywords: ["search"],onSelect: () => find() },
]);

// Render results into the list when they change
palette.results();  // -> [{ id, cmd, score, matches }, ...]
```

## Options

| Option            | Default               | Notes                                                       |
| ----------------- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `triggerKey`      | `{key:"k",meta:true}` | Global keybind. `meta:true` matches Cmd OR Ctrl             |
| `fuzzy`           | `true`                | enable fuzzy (non-contiguous) matching as last-resort tier  |
| `maxResults`      | `50`                  | cap the result list                                         |
| `recentBoost`     | `5`                   | score bonus for recently-invoked commands (within tier)     |
| `rememberRecent`  | `true`                | track invoke history                                         |
| `recentLimit`     | `10`                  | how many recent commands to remember                         |
| `invokeOnSelect`  | `true`                | call `cmd.onSelect` on Enter / click                         |
| `onSelect`        | -                     | `(cmd, source) => ?`                                         |
| `onOpen`          | -                     | `(reason) => ?`                                              |
| `onClose`         | -                     | `(reason) => ?`                                              |
| `onOpenChange`    | -                     | `(isOpen, reason) => ?`                                      |
| `onQueryChange`   | -                     | `(query) => ?`                                               |
| `onResultsChange` | -                     | `(results) => ?` — fires whenever the filtered list changes |
| `onActiveIndexChange` | -                 | `(index) => ?`                                               |

`source` values for invoke: `"click"`, `"keyboard"`, `"invoke"` (default).
`reason` values for open/close: `"keybind"`, `"toggle"`, `"escape"`, `"invoke"`, `"imperative"`.

## Composition with dialog

The palette is INTENTIONALLY not a modal. It owns the command
registry, filtering, keyboard nav, and ARIA combobox wiring. The
modal concerns (backdrop, focus trap, scroll lock) belong to the
dialog primitive. Consumers compose:

```js
palette.onOpen  = () => dialog.open();   // or use onOpenChange
palette.onClose = () => dialog.close();
dialog.onClose  = () => palette.close(); // bidirectional sync
```

Or via the element wrappers, using events:

```html
<lite-dialog id="dlg" modal>
    <lite-command-palette id="palette">
        <input data-cmd-input placeholder="Type a command...">
        <ul data-cmd-list></ul>
        <div data-cmd-empty>No commands found</div>
    </lite-command-palette>
</lite-dialog>

<script>
    const palette = document.getElementById("palette");
    const dialog  = document.getElementById("dlg");
    palette.addEventListener("openchange", e => {
        if (e.detail.open) dialog.open();
        else               dialog.close();
    });
    dialog.addEventListener("closechange", e => {
        if (!e.detail.open) palette.close();
    });
</script>
```

## Command shape

```js
{
    id:        "save",                         // required, unique
    label:     "Save",                         // required, what users see
    keywords?: ["write", "persist"],           // alt search terms
    group?:    "File",                         // category label (consumer-rendered)
    disabled?: false,                          // hide if true
    when?:     () => context.isInEditor,       // dynamic filter
    onSelect?: (cmd, source) => save(),        // invoked on Enter / click
    // any other custom fields (icon, shortcut, ...) are preserved
    // for the consumer to read off result.cmd in onResultsChange
}
```

`when()` is re-evaluated **only** when `palette.refresh()` is called
or when query changes. After mutating external context (e.g.,
focusing a different region of your app), call `palette.refresh()`
to update the filtered list.

## Scoring tiers

The palette ranks each registered command against the query and
returns matches sorted by score (descending). Insertion order
breaks ties. Scoring tiers (highest first):

| Tier              | Score range | Example (query: `"save"`)                          |
| ----------------- | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Exact label match | 100         | `"save"` matches `"Save"`                          |
| Prefix match      | 95          | `"save"` matches `"Save As..."`                    |
| Start-of-word     | 80-89       | `"save"` matches `"Auto Save"` at idx 5            |
| Substring         | 50-65       | `"save"` matches `"Unsaved doc"` at idx 2          |
| Keyword           | 45          | `"write"` matches a command with keyword `"write"` |
| Fuzzy             | 20-30       | `"ocl"` matches `"Open Command Line"`              |

Within a tier, earlier match position scores slightly higher
(`startOfWordMatch` at idx 2 beats `startOfWordMatch` at idx 10).

`recentBoost` adds up to 5 points to recently-invoked commands.
Because the boost is small relative to tier gaps (95 → 50 is 45
points), recents rise within their tier but don't escalate above
higher tiers. Exact matches always win.

Match positions (the character spans that matched) are returned
with each result so the consumer can render highlighted spans:

```js
palette.addEventListener("resultschange", (e) => {
    list.innerHTML = "";
    for (let i = 0; i < e.detail.results.length; i++) {
        const r = e.detail.results[i];
        const li = document.createElement("li");
        li.innerHTML = highlight(r.cmd.label, r.matches);  // <b>...</b> ranges
        list.appendChild(li);
        palette.markItem(li, r.id, i);    // <-- IMPORTANT: register for click + active state
    }
});
```

## Attachment methods

| Method                | Notes                                                              |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `attachInput(el)`     | role=combobox, aria-expanded follows open state, aria-controls=list |
| `attachList(el)`      | role=listbox; delegates click + hover                              |
| `attachEmpty(el)`     | toggles `[hidden]` based on result count                            |
| `markItem(el, id, i)` | wrapper helper — call for each rendered <li>; sets role=option, etc |

## Imperative API

```js
palette.register(cmd)              // single command
palette.register([cmd1, cmd2, ...]) // bulk
palette.unregister(id)
palette.clear()
palette.refresh()                  // re-evaluate when() filters

palette.open(reason?)
palette.close(reason?)
palette.toggle(reason?)

palette.setQuery(q)
palette.invoke(id, source?)
palette.invokeActive(source?)
palette.setActive(idx)
palette.next() / palette.prev()

// reactive accessors
palette.isOpen()
palette.query()
palette.results()                  // current filtered + ranked
palette.activeIndex()
palette.commands()

palette.destroy()
```

## Keyboard

On the input (when focused):

| Key                | Action                                  |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------- |
| ArrowDown / Ctrl+J / Ctrl+N | next result (wraps)            |
| ArrowUp / Ctrl+K / Ctrl+P   | prev result (wraps)            |
| Home               | first result                            |
| End                | last result                             |
| Enter              | invoke active                           |
| Escape             | close                                   |
| Cmd+K (or trigger) | close (toggling out)                    |

Global (anywhere on the page):

| Key                | Action                                  |
| ------------------ | --------------------------------------- |
| triggerKey         | toggle palette open/closed              |

The global keybind is INPUT-CONTEXT AWARE — pressing Cmd+K while
focus is in another `<input>`, `<textarea>`, `<select>`, or
contenteditable does NOT open the palette. (Exception: the
palette's own input gets the keybind because it's already the
target. That's how Cmd+K can close the palette while you're
typing in it.)

To disable the global keybind, pass `triggerKey: null` (or attribute
`trigger-key="none"` on the wrapper).

## Custom element wrapper

```html
<lite-command-palette trigger-key="Cmd+K" max-results="50">
    <input data-cmd-input placeholder="Type a command..." />
    <ul   data-cmd-list></ul>
    <div  data-cmd-empty>No commands found</div>
</lite-command-palette>

<script type="module">
    import "@zakkster/lite-headless/command-palette/element";

    const palette = document.querySelector("lite-command-palette");

    palette.register([
        { id: "save",    label: "Save",    keywords: ["write"], onSelect: save },
        { id: "open",    label: "Open",                         onSelect: openFile },
        { id: "find",    label: "Find",    keywords: ["search"], onSelect: find },
        { id: "format",  label: "Format Document", group: "Edit", onSelect: format },
    ]);

    // render results as they change
    const list = palette.querySelector("[data-cmd-list]");
    palette.addEventListener("resultschange", (e) => {
        list.innerHTML = "";
        e.detail.results.forEach((r, i) => {
            const li = document.createElement("li");
            li.textContent = r.cmd.label;
            list.appendChild(li);
            palette.markItem(li, r.id, i);
        });
    });

    // initial paint
    palette.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("resultschange", { detail: { results: palette.results } }));
</script>
```

### Attributes

| Attribute       | Effect                                                              |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `trigger-key`   | `"Cmd+K"` (default), `"Ctrl+/"`, `"Cmd+Shift+P"`, `"none"`, or JSON  |
| `max-results`   | numeric cap (default 50)                                            |
| `no-fuzzy`      | disable fuzzy tier (presence)                                       |
| `no-recent`     | disable recent-boost (presence)                                     |
| `recent-limit`  | how many recent commands to track (default 10)                      |

### Dispatched events

- `openchange`    — `{ detail: { open, reason } }`
- `select`        — `{ detail: { command, source } }`
- `querychange`   — `{ detail: { query } }`
- `resultschange` — `{ detail: { results } }`
- `activeindexchange` — `{ detail: { index } }`

## When NOT to use

- **Form select / autocomplete** — use the `combobox` primitive,
  which is designed for picking a single value into a form field.
- **Single-trigger menu** — use the `menu` primitive (right-click
  menus, dropdown menus from a button).
- **Just a fuzzy filter input** — if you don't need invocation,
  global keybinding, or recent-boost, a plain `<input>` + filtered
  list will do.

The command palette is specifically for: **a global, searchable
registry of INVOCABLE actions accessed via a keyboard shortcut**.
That's the niche.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
